prose:
[1] The Alcyone lurched violently as a sudden gust of wind caught its sails, sending the crew scrambling to secure loose ropes. Elena gripped the rail, her knuckles white, as the deck tilted beneath her feet. The ship groaned against the force of the waves, and the horizon blurred into a churning gray mass. Ahead, the jagged silhouette of an island emerged, its peaks shrouded in mist. The crew shouted orders, their voices barely audible over the howling wind, as they adjusted the sails to steer the brig toward the looming landmass.

[2] “We’re too close to the shallows!” Yusuf barked, his voice sharp as he grabbed the helm from the helmsman. Captain Reeve stepped forward, his face grim. “You’ve sailed these waters before,” he said, his tone edged with accusation. Yusuf’s jaw tightened, but he didn’t respond. Elena, clutching her brother’s notebook, interjected, “If we don’t find shelter soon, we’ll be dashed against the rocks.” Reeve glanced at her, then back at Yusuf. “Then guide us in,” he ordered. Yusuf nodded curtly and turned his focus to the storm.

[3] The Alcyone plunged through the waves, its bow slicing into the froth as Yusuf expertly navigated the narrow channel. The crew worked frantically, their hands numb from the cold spray as they hauled lines and adjusted sails. Elena clung to the mast, her eyes fixed on the island’s dark cliffs, searching for any sign of shelter. The wind howled louder, carrying with it the acrid scent of sulfur from the volcanic peaks. The ship shuddered as it entered the calmer waters of a hidden cove, the storm’s fury momentarily muted by the towering rock walls.

[4] “This cove,” Elena said, her voice trembling with relief, “it’s not on the charts.” Yusuf’s expression remained unreadable. “No,” he replied shortly. Reeve crossed his arms, his gaze piercing. “You knew it was here,” he said. Yusuf shrugged. “I’ve seen it before. Doesn’t mean I like it.” Elena frowned, clutching her notebook tighter. “Could the Meridian have taken shelter here?” she asked. Yusuf hesitated, then shook his head. “Doubt it. This place… it’s not safe for long.” Reeve’s eyes narrowed. “Explain,” he demanded.

[5] Elena’s mind raced as she scanned the cove’s shadowed edges. The notebook in her hands felt heavier, its pages filled with her brother’s cryptic notes and sketches. Could he have been here? The thought sent a shiver down her spine. She glanced at Yusuf, his silence unnerving. What wasn’t he saying? The cove’s stillness was unnatural, the air thick with an oppressive weight. She couldn’t shake the feeling they were being watched, though she saw nothing but the jagged rocks and the dark water lapping at the ship’s hull.

[6] “What’s not safe about this place?” Reeve pressed, stepping closer to Yusuf. The quartermaster’s eyes darted to the cliffs above. “Things happen here,” he muttered. “Things you wouldn’t believe.” Elena stepped forward, her curiosity overriding her fear. “What kind of things?” Yusuf shook his head. “Not now. Not here.” Reeve’s voice hardened. “If there’s something you’re not telling us, Yusuf, now’s the time.” Yusuf’s gaze flickered to Elena, then back to Reeve. “You wouldn’t understand,” he said quietly.

[7] “Try me,” Reeve snapped, his patience fraying. Yusuf exhaled sharply. “I’ve seen ships vanish in these waters,” he said. “Not wrecked. Vanished. No wreckage, no survivors. Just… gone.” Elena’s breath caught. “The Meridian?” she asked, her voice barely above a whisper. Yusuf shrugged. “Could be. Or something else.” Reeve’s jaw tightened. “And you didn’t think to mention this before?” Yusuf’s eyes flashed. “Would you have believed me?” he shot back. Reeve didn’t answer, his silence heavy.

[8] “We need to explore,” Elena said, breaking the tension. “If there’s any chance the Meridian was here—” Reeve cut her off. “Not in this storm,” he said. “We’ll wait it out.” Yusuf nodded reluctantly. “Aye. But keep your eyes open. And your weapons close.” Elena frowned. “What are we looking for?” Yusuf’s expression darkened. “Anything that doesn’t belong,” he said cryptically. Reeve’s hand instinctively went to the pistol at his belt. “Enough riddles,” he growled. “Speak plainly.” Yusuf shook his head. “You’ll see soon enough.”

[9] The crew exchanged uneasy glances as the ship settled into the eerie calm of the cove. Elena clutched her notebook, her heart pounding. “What if we’re not alone?” she asked, her voice trembling. Yusuf’s gaze drifted to the cliffs again. “We’re not,” he said simply. Reeve’s grip tightened on his pistol. “Then we’ll be ready,” he said, though his voice lacked conviction. Elena stared into the shadows, the weight of Yusuf’s words settling over her like a shroud. The storm outside raged on, but the cove’s silence was far more terrifying.

But keep your eyes open. And your weapons close.” Elena frowned. “What are we looking for?” Yusuf’s expression darkened. “Anything that doesn’t belong,” he said cryptically. Reeve’s hand instinctively went to the pistol at his belt. “Enough riddles,” he growled. “Speak plainly.” Yusuf shook his head. “You’ll see soon enough.”

The crew exchanged uneasy glances as the ship settled into the eerie calm of the cove. Elena clutched her notebook, her heart pounding. “What if we’re not alone?” she asked, her voice trembling. Yusuf’s gaze drifted to the cliffs again. “We’re not,” he said simply. Reeve’s grip tightened on his pistol. “Then we’ll be ready,” he said, though his voice lacked conviction. Elena stared into the shadows, the weight of Yusuf’s words settling over her like a shroud. The storm outside raged on, but the cove’s silence was far more terrifying.
